meta_title: For Students: portfolio building to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Portfolio Building to Solve Debugging Issues
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Learn how portfolio building with Antigravity and Claude Code turns debugging challenges into high-value career assets.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't a hurdle; it's a diagnostic skill. By integrating Antigravity for automated analysis and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you transform code failures into portfolio-worthy case studies. This systematic approach documents your problem-solving logic, proving your technical competence to future employers while eliminating the frustration of stalled development cycles.
content.intro:
The blinking cursor of a broken terminal is the rite of passage every developer knows. You've spent hours staring at a stack trace, feeling the weight of a project that refuses to run. It feels like a wall, but in reality, it is the most critical juncture in your growth as a software engineer.

True mastery isn't about writing bug-free code; it's about the resilience to dismantle complex failures. By pivoting from passive debugging to active portfolio building, you stop viewing errors as roadblocks and start treating them as documentation of your technical depth. Let's turn your debugging sessions into the foundation of your professional career.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure State
steps.description: Before fixing, define the exact boundary of the error. Use Antigravity to generate a diagnostic map of the failing module.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the attached codebase and generate a dependency tree focused on the failing module. Identify the specific function calls leading to the crash.

steps.title: Document the Root Cause
steps.description: Turn the bug into a learning artifact. Capture the error, the hypothesis, and the eventual discovery.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a technical summary of this bug, explaining the root cause in simple, professional terms suitable for a portfolio case study.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write --file docs/post-mortem.md --summary 'Explain the logic error in the authentication flow'

steps.title: Execute Targeted Refactoring
steps.description: Apply the fix using iterative cycles to ensure no regression in other parts of the application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose a refactor for this specific function to improve error handling and include robust logging for future debugging.

steps.title: Validate with Automated Testing
steps.description: Ensure the fix holds by creating a unit test that specifically targets the previous failure point.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a pytest suite that replicates the edge case that caused the original crash to ensure it never returns.

steps.title: Publish the Success Story
steps.description: Add this fix to your portfolio as a 'Problem Solved' entry to demonstrate real-world engineering skills.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a README snippet for my portfolio describing how I solved this complex concurrency issue, highlighting the tools and logic used.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit --message 'Add documentation for bugfix #102: Refactored async handling'

pro_tips: Treat every error message as a free mentorship session; ask the AI to explain the 'why' behind the syntax, not just the fix.
Always maintain a 'Bugs & Breakthroughs' markdown file in your repo; it is the most impressive document you can show during an interview.
Use Claude Code to generate unit tests *before* you fix the bug to prove you understand the expected output.
If the code still fails, ask the AI to 'simulate a code review' from a Senior Engineer's perspective to find hidden assumptions.
Automate your documentation updates using commit hooks so your portfolio is always current without manual effort.
resources: Writing Case Studies for Developers: https://dev.to/portfolio-guide
content.next_steps: Select one complex bug from your previous projects, apply the 5-step framework, and publish the documentation to your GitHub README.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped to turn every 'can't debug' moment into a professional win. Don't let your code errors define your limits—use them to build your reputation. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
